paper_title: STARE: Surprisal-Guided Token-Level Advantage Reweighting for Policy Entropy Stability

problem_reconstruction.original_problem: GRPO-based RLVR training suffers from policy entropy collapse, limiting the number of stable training steps and causing premature convergence.
problem_reconstruction.reconstructed_problem: The entropy collapse in GRPO stems from a token-level credit assignment mismatch: GRPO assigns the same trajectory-level advantage Āᵢ to all tokens in a rollout, but the per-token entropy effect decomposes into the product of Āᵢ and a local entropy sensitivity function. Low-surprisal tokens dominate sampling frequency under the current policy, creating a systematic asymmetry where entropy-decreasing updates overwhelm entropy-increasing ones. The true question is not just 'how to slow entropy decay' but 'which tokens drive the collapse and how minimal an intervention can reverse it'.
problem_reconstruction.key_insight: The entropy neutrality identity (Theorem 3.4) combined with the near-criticality property (Corollary 3.6) reveals that entropy collapse requires only a tiny weight perturbation to reverse — the system operates near a critical point where W*−1 = O(T⁻¹), meaning a 1-5% reweighting of specific token subsets suffices.
problem_reconstruction.gap_addressed: Prior work operated at trajectory or sample granularity with coarse entropy control. STARE provides a principled, theoretically grounded token-level mechanism targeting exactly the entropy-critical tokens.

methodology_reconstruction.core_approach: First-order gradient analysis of token-level entropy dynamics under GRPO → Mathematical decomposition into four-quadrant advantage-surprisal structure → Identification of critical tokens via batch-internal surprisal quantiles → Token-level advantage reweighting → Closed-loop target-entropy gating for stable regulation.

branches.name: Token partitioning (Section 4.1)
branches.detail: Batch-internal top-P% surprisal quantiles within T⁺ and T⁻

branches.name: Weight scheduling (Section 4.4)
branches.detail: Fixed vs adaptive: W_{k+1} = clip(W_k + α·sgn(H_tgt − Ĥ_k), [1, W_max])

theoretical_tools: Entropy gradient w.r.t. logits: ∂H/∂z_v = p_v(s_v − H)
Entropy sensitivity function: F(p) = p(ln p + H) − S₂ where S₂ = Σ_v p_v²(ln p_v + H)
GRPO logit update: Δz_v = h·Ā(δ_{va} − p_v)
evaluation_metrics: Policy entropy (position-level H = −Σ_v p_v ln p_v = E_p[s])
AMC23, MATH-500, Minerva Math, OlympiadBench accuracy (avg@N, N=4 for non-AIME)
key_assumptions: Unclipped regime analysis (theoretical analysis; practical implementation uses clipping)
Sufficiently long sequences (T large) and large batch sizes for near-criticality
On-policy updates with single gradient step per batch

key_findings.finding: Entropy collapse in GRPO is caused by a systematic token-level credit assignment mismatch — low-surprisal tokens dominate while high-surprisal entropy-increasing tokens are diluted.
key_findings.significance: Provides the first principled mechanism explanation of entropy collapse, moving beyond empirical observations.

key_findings.finding: The system exhibits near-criticality: a minimal weight perturbation (W*−1 = O(T⁻¹)) near 1% suffices to flip entropy evolution direction.
key_findings.significance: Explains why previous crude interventions often overshoot — the critical point is very close to identity, and beyond it only magnitude (not direction) changes.

key_findings.finding: Batch-internal top-10% surprisal quantiles reliably approximate the theoretical critical threshold (consistency 60%→95% over training).
key_findings.significance: Validates a simple, computationally efficient proxy for identifying entropy-critical tokens without per-position threshold computation.

key_findings.finding: STARE consistently outperforms DAPO by 4-8% on AIME24/25 across 1.5B-32B scales and three task scenarios.
key_findings.significance: Demonstrates that token-level credit rebalancing is more effective than trajectory-level heuristics for entropy management.

key_findings.finding: STARE sustains over 5000 RL training steps while GRPO-ds saturates around step 1000.
key_findings.significance: Unlocks long-horizon RL potential that was previously capped by entropy collapse.

key_findings.finding: Reweighted tokens concentrate on self-reflection and uncertainty vocabulary.
key_findings.significance: Suggests that entropy maintenance indirectly promotes beneficial exploratory behaviors (self-correction, re-evaluation).

limitations.limitation: Theoretical analysis limited to first-order gradient approximation in the unclipped regime.
limitations.impact: May not capture higher-order effects and clipping-region dynamics present in actual training.

limitations.limitation: Target entropy H_tgt requires manual setting.
limitations.impact: Adds one hyperparameter; though experiments show robustness to its value within reasonable range.

limitations.limitation: Evaluation primarily on mathematical reasoning benchmarks.
limitations.impact: Generalization to code generation, scientific reasoning, and other domains unverified.
Behavior on larger models (70B+) remains unclear.

limitations.limitation: H_tgt = 0.3 may not be universally optimal.
limitations.impact: Different tasks or domains may require different target entropy levels.

limitations.limitation: Static P% = 10% is a fixed hyperparameter.
limitations.impact: May not be optimal across all training phases and model sizes.

future_directions_suggested: Extend theoretical analysis to clipped regime and higher-order effects
Develop adaptive, threshold-free methods for identifying entropy-critical tokens
Apply STARE to more diverse domains (code, science, multi-modal)
Integrate with per-token reward decomposition for finer-grained credit assignment
Scale to larger models (70B+) and longer training horizons
Explore dynamic P scheduling during training
Transfer the four-quadrant analytical framework to other RLHF algorithms (PPO, etc.)
